sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Inter are in strong form, having secured 4 wins and 1 draw in their last 5 matches, showcasing their attacking prowess with 10 goals scored. Parma, on the other hand, have struggled recently, managing only 2 wins and 2 draws in their last 5 games, with a modest 4 goals. This disparity highlights Inter's dominance heading into this clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, Inter have remained unbeaten, with a 3-1 victory and a 2-2 draw. Historically, Inter have won 3 of the last 5 head-to-heads, with 2 draws, and both teams often score in these matches, as seen in 7 of the last 8 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Inter lead the Serie A standings with 39 points and 38 goals scored, boasting a potent attack led by Lautaro Martínez (10 goals). Parma, positioned 15th with 18 points, have struggled offensively with just 12 goals, though their defense has been relatively solid, conceding 19 goals.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Inter’s Lautaro Martínez and Hakan Çalhanoğlu are in top form, contributing significantly to their goal tally and creativity. For Parma, Mateo Pellegrino has shown promise with 5 goals, but their overall attacking output remains limited compared to Inter’s firepower.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Inter’s high possession (around 58-64% in recent games) and ability to create big chances (averaging 3-9 per game) could overwhelm Parma’s defense. Parma’s low possession (42% average) and fewer corners (3.4 per game) suggest they might rely on counter-attacks or set pieces to score.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Inter’s superior form, league position, and historical edge, they are favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 57% away win probability. Parma will need to capitalize on limited chances while containing Inter’s relentless attack to avoid a defeat at home.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Inter leads Parma 1-0 with a goal from Federico Dimarco in the 42nd minute at the Ennio Tardini Stadium. Inter has dominated much of the game, showing superior control and creating more dangerous opportunities. Parma, on the other hand, has struggled to find their rhythm and failed to register any significant threats in front of goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Inter's performance in the first half was characterized by strong ball possession and frequent entries into the final third, which allowed them to maintain pressure on Parma's defense. They created a big chance and converted it through Dimarco, while also hitting the woodwork multiple times, indicating their attacking intent. Parma, however, showed limited presence in the opposition's penalty area and lacked the cutting edge to create clear-cut chances. Their defensive efforts were tested repeatedly, and they were fortunate to concede only once before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half was overwhelmingly in favor of Inter, who consistently built pressure with sustained attacking spells from the opening minutes. They had several periods of dominance, particularly in the middle of the half, where they pushed forward with intent and forced Parma onto the back foot. Parma showed brief moments of promise with a slight shift in momentum during isolated minutes, but these were quickly neutralized by Inter's relentless waves of attack, culminating in the goal just before half-time.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Inter, Federico Dimarco has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also being heavily involved in creating opportunities. His ability to deliver from wide areas could be crucial in the second half. Lautaro Martínez, despite missing a big chance, remains a constant threat and will likely look to get on the scoresheet. On Parma's side, Jacob Ondrejka showed some potential to create danger, and his contribution could be vital if Parma hopes to turn things around. Emanuele Valeri also stood out defensively and in creating play, offering a possible outlet for counter-attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Parma will need to adjust their approach in the second half, focusing on tightening their defensive structure while seeking to exploit any gaps on the counter. They must improve their final third presence to have any chance of equalizing. Inter, meanwhile, is likely to continue their high-pressing game, looking to capitalize on their dominance and secure the result with another goal. They may push for an early strike in the second half to kill off the game, potentially targeting Parma's vulnerabilities in aerial duels.
